answer:
The refund depends on whether the policy is canceled by the insured or the insurer. For insured-initiated cancellation, the refund is typically on a short-rate basis, while for carrier-initiated cancellation, it is on a pro-rata basis. Using standard short-rate tables, the refund for 90 days (about 25% of the year) would be approximately $26 for the insured and $30.14 for the carrier.

\boxed{A}
